name: GMS Scrape Start (Apollo lead pipeline, part 1)

parameters.content:
# Part 1 - Scrape Start

Kicks off a Google Maps scrape and wires it to a webhook callback so you never poll or guess a wait time. When the scrape finishes, Apify calls **Part 2** (the enrichment workflow) automatically.

## Flow
1. Form submission: vertical, search terms (comma-separated), location, max per search.
2. Insert a `cold_outreach.gms_runs` row, capture the run id.
3. Build a base64 `webhooks` param and POST the Apify Google Maps actor. Apify fires `WEBHOOK_BASE/webhook/maps-lead-enrich-inbound` on finish, embedding the run id so Part 2 joins back to the right row.
4. Update the row with the Apify run id.
5. Show a `queued` completion page.

## Before activating
- Attach a **Postgres** credential (any Postgres 12+). Apply `sql/schema.sql` first.
- Attach an **Apify HTTP Query Auth** credential (sends your token as the `token` query param).
- Set `WEBHOOK_BASE` in the **Config** node to your public n8n URL, e.g. `https://your-n8n.example.com`. The `/webhook/maps-lead-enrich-inbound` path must be reachable by Apify.
- Import **Part 2** (`apollo-lead-enrichment.json`) and activate it too, so the callback has somewhere to land.

parameters.formDescription: Triggers a Google Maps scrape and routes results through Apollo enrichment + ICP scoring. Results do not appear in this form; check your leads table (or the read-only viewer in viewer/) when the run finishes.

values.placeholder: Estate Planning Attorney, Elder Law, Family Law

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "includeWebResults": true,
  "language": "en",
  "locationQuery": "{{ $('Parse form').first().json.location_query }}",
  "maxCrawledPlacesPerSearch": {{ $('Parse form').first().json.max_per_search }},
  "maxImages": 0,
  "scrapeContacts": true,
  "scrapeDirectories": false,
  "scrapeImageAuthors": false,
  "scrapePlaceDetailPage": true,
  "scrapeReviewsPersonalData": false,
  "scrapeTableReservationProvider": false,
  "searchStringsArray": {{ JSON.stringify($('Parse form').first().json.search_terms) }},
  "skipClosedPlaces": true,
  "website": "allPlaces"
}

parameters.query:
UPDATE cold_outreach.gms_runs
SET apify_run_id = $1, apify_dataset_id = $2, status = 'running'
WHERE id = $3
RETURNING id, apify_run_id;

parameters.completionMessage: =Run #{{ $('Insert gms_runs').first().json.id }} is running on Apify. Results land in your cold-lead table when enrichment finishes; open the read-only viewer (viewer/) to watch them arrive. Verticals queue up independently.

parameters.content:
## Section 1
Input, persist the run, kick off Apify with a webhook callback embedded.

The **Build webhooks param** node is the magic: it base64-encodes a `webhooks` array and passes it as a query param on the Apify run POST. Apify fires `WEBHOOK_BASE/webhook/maps-lead-enrich-inbound` when the run finishes, carrying `run_pk` so Part 2 joins back to the correct DB row. No polling, no guessed wait times.
